# Skylogix Aviation
> FAA-compliant flight school at Brackett Field (KPOC), La Verne, California. Affordable Cessna 152 training from Private Pilot License to Airline Transport Pilot.

## About
Skylogix Aviation provides high-quality, affordable flight training in well-maintained Cessna 152 aircraft. Our mission is to make aviation accessible while maintaining the highest safety standards. We are dedicated to helping students achieve their goal of becoming airline transport pilots.

## Location
- Address: 1395 Fairplex Dr. Hangar D4, La Verne, CA 91750
- Airport: Brackett Field Airport (KPOC)
- Region: Los Angeles County, Southern California

## Contact
- Phone: (562) 266-6868
- Email: info@skylogixaviation.com
- Hours: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM PST
- Website: https://www.skylogixaviation.com

## Pricing
- Aircraft rental: $120/hr (wet rate, fuel included)
- Flight instruction: $55/hr (FAA-certified CFI)
- Typical combined training cost: ~$175–$200/hr

## Fleet
All training aircraft are Cessna 152s — IFR-rated, two-seat trainers:
- N49202 (1977 Cessna 152 II) — Lycoming O-235-L2C, 115 HP, IFR
- N4900L (1980 Cessna 152 II) — Lycoming O-235-L2C, 115 HP, IFR
- N25976 (1978 Cessna 152 II) — Lycoming O-235-L2C, 115 HP, IFR

## Training Programs

### Private Pilot License (PPL)
- Minimum age: 17
- Minimum flight hours: 40 (20 dual, 10 solo)
- Requirements: FAA Knowledge Test, Checkride, 3rd-Class Medical Certificate
- Duration: Typically 3–6 months with consistent training

### Instrument Rating (IFR)
- Prerequisite: PPL
- Minimum hours: 50 cross-country PIC, 40 instrument hours
- Requirements: Instrument Knowledge Test, Checkride

### Commercial Pilot License (CPL)
- Minimum age: 18
- Prerequisite: PPL + IFR
- Minimum hours: 250 total flight hours
- Requirements: Commercial Knowledge Test, Checkride

### Certified Flight Instructor (CFI)
- Prerequisite: CPL
- Requirements: FOI Knowledge Test, CFI Knowledge Test, CFI Checkride
- Benefit: Earn income while building flight hours toward ATP

### Airline Transport Pilot (ATP)
- Minimum age: 23
- Prerequisite: CPL + IFR
- Minimum hours: 1,500 total flight hours
- Requirements: ATP Knowledge Test, Checkride
- This is the highest level of pilot certification, required to serve as airline captain

## Common Questions

Q: How long to get a PPL?
A: Most students complete their Private Pilot License in 3–6 months training consistently.

Q: What is a discovery flight?
A: An introductory lesson where you fly a real aircraft with a CFI. The best way to experience aviation before committing to full training.

Q: Do I need a medical certificate?
A: Yes. PPL requires a 3rd-Class FAA Medical Certificate. Commercial and ATP require higher classes.

Q: What area do you serve?
A: We serve La Verne, Pomona, Claremont, Ontario, Upland, and the wider Los Angeles / Inland Empire region.
